texto: Within the moment of now, there is presence of ever there, As It is;, Invisible; Nothingness; of Still and silent awareness; in substratum; witnessing itself which U in real R...It has nothing to do with Your mind, Your body appearance and Your world...Wisdom is to let it recognize and and realize itself...Often in Ignorance of IT, ignorant start believing "In the Moment", "As It Is" words, which point towards IT (awareness), ignorantly for the appearing body, appearing mind and appearing creation...Be Happy and Keep Smiling...

texto: Remaining aware of awareness, witnessing it thru every experience within without one, is the way and the goal both...Be Happy and keep smiling...

texto: We must realize that violence is not confined to physical violence. Fear is violence, caste discrimination is violence, exploitation of others, however subtle, is violence, segregation is violence, thinking ill of others and condemning others are violence. In order to reduce individual acts of physical violence, we must work to eliminate violence at all levels, mental, verbal, personal, and social, including violence to animals, plants, and all other forms of life.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Satish Kumar sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. A afirmação “We must realize that violence is not confined to physical violence. Fear is violence, caste discr…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, discrimination, exploitation, útil em momentos de trabalho e exploitation.

texto: If you can kill animals, the same attitude can kill human beings. The mentality is the same which exploits nature and which creates wars.
explicacao: Citação de Satish Kumar sobre temas ligados a saude mental. “If you can kill animals, the same attitude can kill human beings. The mentality is the same which…” articula saude-mental, nature, animals em registro de saude mental, com relevância para contextos como animals e you.

texto: Someone, somewhere, needs to take courage to break the cycle of violence. Forgiveness is superior to justice. Being kind and compassionate to those who are good to you is easy. True forgiveness and compassion come only when one is able to forgive even those who have committed barbaric acts. If Angulimala is capable of renouncing violence, then tell me, your Majesty: is your civilized society also capable of being truly civilized and renouncing violence?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Satish Kumar que provoca enfrentar incertezas com ação. Ao questionar “Someone, somewhere, needs to take courage to break the cycle of violence. Forgiveness is superior…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre coragem, buddha, compassion, com foco em compassion e forgiveness.
